title: Hybe - Wikipedia

### Hybe India

On September 23, 2025, Hybe established its Indian subsidiary, Hybe India Entertainment Pvt. Ltd., based in Mumbai. The company aims to develop local talent through auditions and training programs, connecting artists born in India to global audiences. Hybe described Mumbai as “a cultural and entertainment hub that encompasses the Bollywood film industry, contemporary art, and diverse performing arts,” unveiling the slogan “where indian voices become global stories.” Through its activities in India, the subsidiary is also expected to contribute to the growing I-pop scene, a developing segment of the Indian music industry.

## Former artists [...] Hybe Co., Ltd. (Korean: 하이브), commonly known as simply Hybe (stylized in all caps), is a South Korean multinational conglomerate Hybe has transformed from a traditional music entertainment company (formerly Big Hit Entertainment) into a tech-driven lifestyle platform corporation.

The company operates under three main pillars: Labels, Solutions, and Platforms, with a heavy emphasis on utilizing technology for growth.

## History

### 2005–2021: Big Hit Entertainment Co., Ltd. [...] Big Hit announced its rebranding into an entertainment lifestyle platform company under the name Hybe in March 2021. The company underwent a complete organizational restructuring, which also saw the recording and management arm of Big Hit Entertainment spun off into an independent label named Big Hit Music under Hybe's new Labels division. The renaming was approved at a shareholders meeting on March 30. The company relocated to its new headquarters in the Yongsan Trade Center in Yongsan District on March 22, and the rebrand went into effect on March 31. Hybe acquired Scooter Braun's Ithaca Holdings and all its properties, including SB Projects and Big Machine Label Group, through its subsidiary Hybe America in April. The corporation invested $950 million (₩1.07 trillion) into the America

**Hybe Co., Ltd.**[[1]](#cite_note-Company_established-1) ([Korean](/wiki/Korean_language "Korean language"): 하이브), commonly known as simply **Hybe** (stylized in [all caps](/wiki/All_caps "All caps")), is a South Korean [multinational](/wiki/Multinational_corporation "Multinational corporation") conglomerate[[6]](#cite_note-8) Hybe has transformed from a traditional music entertainment company (formerly Big Hit Entertainment) into a tech-driven lifestyle platform corporation.

The company operates under three main pillars: Labels, Solutions, and Platforms, with a heavy emphasis on utilizing technology for growth.

## History

### 2005–2021: Big Hit Entertainment Co., Ltd.

Big Hit Entertainment was founded on February 1, 2005,[[7]](#cite_note-9) and signed the vocal trio [8Eight](/wiki/8Eight "8Eight") in 2007.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-10) In 2010, the company signed a contract with [JYP Entertainment](/wiki/JYP_Entertainment "JYP Entertainment") to jointly manage the boy group [2AM](/wiki/2AM_(band) "2AM (band)").[[9]](#cite_note-:2-11) That year, Bang Si-hyuk signed [RM](/wiki/RM_(rapper) "RM (rapper)") as the first member of [BTS](/wiki/BTS "BTS") and launched nationwide auditions to recruit other members for the group.[[10]](#cite_note-12) BTS made their debut under Big Hit on June 13, 2013.[[11]](#cite_note-13)

In 2012, the company signed [Lim Jeong-hee](/wiki/Lim_Jeong-hee "Lim Jeong-hee"),[[12]](#cite_note-:1-14) and formed the girl group [Glam](/wiki/GLAM_(band) "GLAM (band)") as a collaboration with [Source Music](/wiki/Source_Music "Source Music").[[13]](#cite_note-15) The group was active until 2014, when it was disbanded due to legal actions against one of its members Kim Da-hee. Kim was sentenced to prison after being found guilty of blackmailing actor [Lee Byung-hun](/wiki/Lee_Byung-hun "Lee Byung-hun").[[14]](#cite_note-16)[[15]](#cite_note-17)[[16]](#cite_note-18)

Following the end of the joint contract between Big Hit and JYP in April 2014, three members of 2AM returned to JYP. [Lee Chang-min](/wiki/Lee_Chang-min_(singer) "Lee Chang-min (singer)") remained with Big Hit to continue with his solo career and as part of the duo [Homme](/wiki/Homme_(duo) "Homme (duo)").[[9]](#cite_note-:2-11) 8Eight disbanded later that year, after Baek Chan and Joo Hee's contracts with Big Hit ended.[[8]](#cite_note-:0-10)

In May 2015, Lim parted ways with the agency, following the expiration of her three-year contract,[[12]](#cite_note-:1-14) and [Signal Entertainment Group](/wiki/Signal_Entertainment_Group "Signal Entertainment Group"), a [KOSDAQ](/wiki/KOSDAQ "KOSDAQ")-listed company specializing in artist management and television production, acquired stakes in Big Hit through a [KR₩](/wiki/South_Korean_won "South Korean won")6 billion [convertible bond](/wiki/Convertible_bond "Convertible bond").[[17]](#cite_note-edaily-19) In early 2016, Big Hit ended their stake relationship with Signal and made a full settlement of the bonds.[[18]](#cite_note-20)[[19]](#cite_note-21)

In February 2018, Homme disbanded after member Changmin's contract ended. He left the company to start his own agency, while [Lee Hyun](/wiki/Lee_Hyun "Lee Hyun") continued on as a solo artist.[[20]](#cite_note-Lee_Hyun_now_solo-22) In August, Big Hit and [CJ ENM](/wiki/CJ_ENM_Entertainment_Division "CJ ENM Entertainment Division") released information stating plans to create a joint entertainment agency, with ownership split 52% to the latter and 48% to the former.[[21]](#cite_note-23)[[22]](#cite_note-24) Filed under the name Belift Lab, the agency debuted its first boy group [Enhypen](/wiki/Enhypen "Enhypen") in November 2020.[[23]](#cite_note-25) In October, BTS renewed and extended their contract with the agency for seven more years.[[24]](#cite_note-26) Big Hit was voted Best Investment Company of the Year at the 2018 Korea VC Awards in December.[[25]](#cite_note-27)

Big Hit debuted its second male group, [Tomorrow X Together](/wiki/TXT_(band) "TXT (band)") (TXT), in March 2019.[[26]](#cite_note-28) Also that month, former [CBO](/wiki/Chief_business_officer "Chief business officer") Lenzo Yoon was appointed co-CEO alongside Bang.[[27]](#cite_note-29) Yoon would focus on the business components of the company, while Bang's focus would be on creative production.[[28]](#cite_note-30) In July 2019, the company acquired [Source Music](/wiki/Source_Music "Source Music"),[[29]](#cite_note-31) and in August, the video game firm Superb.[[30]](#cite_note-32) Thanks to the [Weverse](/wiki/Weverse "Weverse") and Weverse Shop[[c]](#cite_note-34) apps developed by its subsidiary beNX, Big Hit was named the fourth most innovative company of 2020 worldwide by *[Fast Company](/wiki/Fast_Company "Fast Company")*.[[32]](#cite_note-35)

In May 2020, Big Hit became the majority shareholder of [Pledis Entertainment](/wiki/Pledis_Entertainment "Pledis Entertainment"). The label would retain its independence; however, its artists would now receive greater financial support and global promotions.[[33]](#cite_note-36) The [Fair Trade Commission](/wiki/Fair_Trade_Commission_(South_Korea) "Fair Trade Commission (South Korea)") (KFTC) approved the acquisition in October.[[34]](#cite_note-37) Big Hit acquired KOZ Entertainment, a record label founded by rapper [Zico](/wiki/Zico_(rapper) "Zico (rapper)"), the following month.[[35]](#cite_note-Benjamin-38)

In January 2021, news media reported that Big Hit and beNX had invested a combined ₩70 billion (US$63 million) in [YG Plus](/wiki/YG_Plus "YG Plus"), acquiring a 17.9% stake in the company in a merchandising and distribution deal that would see YG Plus' artists join the Weverse social media and shop platforms.[[36]](#cite_note-39)[[37]](#cite_note-40)[[38]](#cite_note-41) [Naver Corporation](/wiki/Naver_Corporation "Naver Corporation") later invested ₩354.8 billion ($321 million) in beNX, acquiring 49% of the subsidiary. In return, it transferred its [V Live](/wiki/V_Live "V Live") video streaming service to the latter for the development of a new integrated fan community platform. BeNX was eventually renamed Weverse Company.[[39]](#cite_note-KJAD_Naver_benx-42) The merger was approved by the KFTC in May.[[40]](#cite_note-43) On February 17, Big Hit and [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group") (UMG) announced a strategic partnership between the two companies to collaborate on various music and technology-related endeavors.[[41]](#cite_note-44) At the forefront of the partnership was a joint venture between Big Hit and [Geffen Records](/wiki/Interscope_Geffen_A%26M_Records "Interscope Geffen A&M Records"), a UMG flagship label, that will debut a global boy group through a new, [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles")-based label following a global audition program set to air in 2022. Big Hit would be responsible for selecting and training the artists, while UMG would be in charge of music production, global distribution, and marketing.[[42]](#cite_note-45) Additionally, other UMG artists were set to join Weverse, already home to [Gracie Abrams](/wiki/Gracie_Abrams "Gracie Abrams"), [New Hope Club](/wiki/New_Hope_Club "New Hope Club"), and Alexander 23.[[43]](#cite_note-46) On February 25, Big Hit announced a ₩4 billion ($3.6 million) investment in a Korea-based AI company, Supertone, that specializes in creating hyper-realistic voices using technology.[[44]](#cite_note-47)

### 2021–present: Hybe, expansions, and investigations

Big Hit announced its rebranding into an entertainment lifestyle platform company under the name Hybe in March 2021.[[45]](#cite_note-48) The company underwent a complete organizational restructuring, which also saw the recording and management arm of Big Hit Entertainment spun off into an independent label named [Big Hit Music](/wiki/Big_Hit_Music "Big Hit Music") under Hybe's new Labels division. The renaming was approved at a shareholders meeting on March 30.[[46]](#cite_note-hybe_presentation-49)[[47]](#cite_note-50) The company relocated to its new headquarters in the Yongsan Trade Center in [Yongsan District](/wiki/Yongsan_District "Yongsan District") on March 22,[[46]](#cite_note-hybe_presentation-49)[[2]](#cite_note-yongsan-4) and the rebrand went into effect on March 31.[[48]](#cite_note-51) Hybe acquired [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun")'s Ithaca Holdings and all its properties, including SB Projects[[d]](#cite_note-52) and [Big Machine Label Group](/wiki/Big_Machine_Records#Imprints "Big Machine Records"), through its subsidiary Hybe America[[e]](#cite_note-Hybe_America-53) in April.[[49]](#cite_note-Variety_Ithaca-54)[[50]](#cite_note-55) The corporation invested $950 million (₩1.07 trillion) into the America branch to finance the buyout;[[51]](#cite_note-56) $1.05 billion was paid to Ithaca shareholders and bondholders.[[52]](#cite_note-57) BH Odyssey Merger Sub, a newly created subsidiary of Hybe America, facilitated the acquisition, and was later dissolved after the deal's finalization.[[53]](#cite_note-KJAD_Ithaca-58)[[54]](#cite_note-59) The Carlyle Group, a minority shareholder and original investor, sold its stake in Ithaca as part of the deal. Braun joined Hybe's board, while [Scott Borchetta](/wiki/Scott_Borchetta "Scott Borchetta") retained his position as CEO of Big Machine Label Group.[[49]](#cite_note-Variety_Ithaca-54)[[55]](#cite_note-60) That same month, *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")* named Hybe one of the "100 Most Influential Companies of 2021", under the "Pioneers" section.[[56]](#cite_note-61)

Japanese streaming platform [Showroom](/wiki/Showroom_(streaming_service) "Showroom (streaming service)") announced a business and capital partnership with Hybe in May 2021 to improve its domestic services and expand access to Japanese content in South Korea, the United States, and globally.[[57]](#cite_note-62)[[58]](#cite_note-63) Hybe was added to the [MSCI](/wiki/MSCI "MSCI") Korea index on May 12, and the index updated on May 28.[[59]](#cite_note-64) Following a second organizational restructuring, Bang resigned as CEO on July 1 to return his focus to music production. He was replaced by Jiwon Park but retained his position as chairman of the board of directors.[[60]](#cite_note-Bang_resigns_CEO-65)[[61]](#cite_note-BSH_resign_MBW-66) In November, Hybe entered into an equity-based partnership with Korean [fintech](/wiki/Financial_technology "Financial technology") company Dunamu to develop a joint venture [NFT](/wiki/Non-fungible_token "Non-fungible token") business for the creation of digital assets.[[62]](#cite_note-67)[[63]](#cite_note-Pulse-68) It acquired a 2.6% stake in the company for ₩500 billion ($423.8 million), while Dunamu received a 5.6% stake in Hybe for ₩700 billion ($590 million).[[63]](#cite_note-Pulse-68)[[64]](#cite_note-69) On November 11, Hybe won a [Red Dot Design Award](/wiki/Red_Dot_Design_Award "Red Dot Design Award") in the Brands & Communication Design category at the 2021 Red Dot Design Awards for its new [corporate identity](/wiki/Corporate_identity "Corporate identity") (CI) design.[[f]](#cite_note-70)[[65]](#cite_note-71) The establishment of a new music label, ADOR,[[g]](#cite_note-73) was announced on November 12. [Chief brand officer](/wiki/Chief_brand_officer "Chief brand officer") (CBO) [Min Hee-jin](/wiki/Min_Hee-jin "Min Hee-jin") was appointed as its president and tasked with overseeing the management of the label's first girl group,[[h]](#cite_note-74) later announced as [NewJeans](/wiki/NewJeans "NewJeans"), which debuted in July 2022.[[67]](#cite_note-75)

In January 2022, Hybe published a series of "original stories" featuring boy groups BTS, Enhypen, and Tomorrow X Together in collaboration with [Webtoon](/wiki/Webtoon_(publisher) "Webtoon (publisher)") and [Wattpad](/wiki/Wattpad "Wattpad") as part of a planned IP expansion.[[68]](#cite_note-76) The BTS comic alone earned over 15 million views within two days of release, becoming the fastest title in Webtoon's history to reach that milestone.[[69]](#cite_note-77) In March, *Fast Company* named Hybe the 11th most innovative company of the year globally on its annual top-50 list[[70]](#cite_note-78)—ranking first in the Media category[[71]](#cite_note-79)—while *Time* included Hybe on its 100 Most Influential Companies list for a second consecutive year.[[72]](#cite_note-80) The company topped the "Leaders" category and was dubbed "The Pop Powerhouse" by the outlet for its acquisitions, expansion into the digital frontier, and transformative strides in the music industry during the pandemic era.[[73]](#cite_note-81)[[74]](#cite_note-82) The following month, Hybe won an [iF Product Design Award](/wiki/IF_Product_Design_Award "IF Product Design Award") for "Company Branding" in the Communication category at the [International Forum Design Awards](/wiki/International_Forum_Design "International Forum Design") held in Germany.[[75]](#cite_note-83) Hybe signed a multi-year deal with [The Walt Disney Company Asia Pacific](/wiki/List_of_assets_owned_by_The_Walt_Disney_Company#The_Walt_Disney_Company_Asia_Pacific "List of assets owned by The Walt Disney Company") in July 2022 to provide content showcasing the Korean music and entertainment industries to a global market. Five programs will be available worldwide on [Disney+](/wiki/Disney%2B "Disney+") and other Disney streaming platforms—a [4K](/wiki/4K_resolution "4K resolution") cinematic film of BTS' [Permission to Dance on Stage concert in Los Angeles](/wiki/Permission_to_Dance_on_Stage#Film "Permission to Dance on Stage"); *[In the Soop: Friendcation](/wiki/In_the_Soop:_Friendcation "In the Soop: Friendcation")*; and *BTS Monuments: Beyond the Star*, a [docuseries](/wiki/Television_documentary "Television documentary") of BTS' history from debut to present set to premiere in 2023. Additional content featuring other Hybe artists will also be released.[[76]](#cite_note-84)[[77]](#cite_note-85)

Hybe became the largest shareholder of [SM Entertainment](/wiki/SM_Entertainment "SM Entertainment") in February 2023, after it acquired founder [Lee Soo-man](/wiki/Lee_Soo-man "Lee Soo-man")'s 14.8% stake in the company for approximately ₩422.8 billion.[[78]](#cite_note-86) The company subsequently acquired [Galaxia SM](/wiki/Galaxia_SM "Galaxia SM")'s 1% stake on March 3.[[79]](#cite_note-87) On March 12, Hybe announced that they no longer planned to own the majority stakes of SM Entertainment, said that the bidding war with [Kakao](/wiki/Kakao "Kakao") could "damage shareholder value."[[80]](#cite_note-88) On March 24, Hybe announced that it will sell its entire stake in SM Entertainment to Kakao by accepting its tender offer launched by Kakao.[[81]](#cite_note-89) However, on March 28, Hybe instead sold half of its stakes in SM to Kakao, leaving it only for 8.81%.[[82]](#cite_note-90) On May 27, 2025, Hybe announced it would sell all of its shares in SM Entertainment to [Tencent Music](/wiki/Tencent_Music "Tencent Music").[[83]](#cite_note-91)[[84]](#cite_note-92)

On May 23, 2023, Hybe signed a music distribution deal with Chinese streaming platform, [Tencent Music](/wiki/Tencent_Music "Tencent Music").[[85]](#cite_note-93)

On August 10, 2023, Hybe announced its acquisition of the remaining 51.5% stake from CJ ENM in Belift Lab, making it as a wholly owned subsidiary.[[86]](#cite_note-hybefully-94)[[87]](#cite_note-enhypenbacker-95)

In November 2023, Hybe confirmed the establishment of its [Latin music](/wiki/Latin_music "Latin music") division, Hybe Latin America, based in [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"), [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), and [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On August 27, 2024, Min was removed from her role as president of ADOR but remained on the company's board of directors and continued producing for NewJeans. Ju Young Kim, Hybe's Chief Human Resources Officer and a member of the board, assumed the position of ADOR's president. Min objected this decision, describing her as a "grave violation of the shareholders' agreement."[[88]](#cite_note-96)

In December 2024, it was revealed that the Financial Supervisory Service (FSS) was investigating whether Chairman Bang Si-hyuk had entered into secret shareholder agreements with STIC Investments, Estone Equity Partners, and New Main Equity without informing other shareholders or the financial regulator. These agreements allegedly allowed him to financially benefit misleading other shareholders into believing that Hybe would not pursue an IPO.[[89]](#cite_note-97) On May 28, 2025, the FSS referred Bang Si-hyuk to the prosecution after concluding that the investigation met the threshold for charges.[[90]](#cite_note-98) The following day, searched Hybe's headquarters connection with allegations of insider trading by a former employee who allegedly used confidential information to profit from acquiring shares in YG Plus, based on prior knowledge of Hybe's planned investment in the company in January 2021.[[91]](#cite_note-99)

On May 28, 2025, Hybe announced the sale of Big Machine Rock to Gebbia Media,[[92]](#cite_note-100) followed the next day by the announcement of establishing a Chinese subsidiary.[[93]](#cite_note-101)[[94]](#cite_note-102)[[95]](#cite_note-103)

On July 1, Hybe announced plans to expand its operations into India,[[96]](#cite_note-104) with the subsidiary establish on September 23.[[97]](#cite_note-105) In the same month, one of Hybe's largest shareholders, [Netmarble](/wiki/Netmarble "Netmarble") considered issuing approximately ₩2,500 billion ($2.5 trillion) in exchangeable bonds (EB), using part of its stake in Hybe as collateral to secure liquidity for repaying loans incurred during its acquisition of the U.S. social casino game company SpinX. While a formal underwriting agreement had not yet been signed, NH Investment & Securities emerged as the likely arranger, and NH Hedge Asset Management was reportedly forming a project fund with institutional investors to support the deal. Netmarble has previously utilized its Hybe holdings for financing, including a 2024 price return swap involving 2.6% equity and a 2023 block trade of approximately 6% of its shares.[[98]](#cite_note-106) However, by August, Netmarble abandoned its plan, a decision attributed to the volatility and sharp decline in Hybe's stock price caused by an "owner risk" scandal involving Hybe's founder and Chairman Bang Si‑hyuk. The funding was intended to pay down debt from Netmarble's acquisition of SpinX (approximately ₩1.6 trillion in debt), which is now reduced to mid‑₩200 billion.[[99]](#cite_note-107)

On August 20, Mirae Asset Securities was preparing to sell the remaining portion of Hybe convertible bonds (CBs) worth approximately 1 trillion KRW, capitalizing on the recent surge in Hybe's stock price, which had risen to around 280,000 KRW—well above the CB conversion price of 218,000 KRW. The CBs, issued in October 2024 as zero-coupon, zero-interest bonds, were initially underwritten mainly by Mirae Asset, but some remained unsold due to concerns over the deal's structure and perceived favoritism. With Hybe's stock boosted by positive market sentiment, including anticipation of BTS's return and the easing of Chinese content restrictions, Mirae Asset sees a lucrative opportunity to offload the remaining CBs at a premium, maximizing returns through both underwriting fees and capital gains.[[100]](#cite_note-108) The same day, Hybe subsidiary ADOR appointed Lee Do-kyung as its new president. This leadership change follows the dismissal of former president Min Hee-jin in 2024 and a period of interim leadership under Kim Joo-young, who focused on stabilizing the company and restructuring operations. With that process complete, Hybe determined that Lee—who has been at Hybe since 2019 and has experience in strategic planning and IP projects—was the right person to lead ADOR into its next phase.[[101]](#cite_note-109)

On December 15, Hybe announced a new global management partnership with Brandon Hixon and Colin Gayle, the co-managers of South African artist [Tyla](/wiki/Tyla "Tyla"), to develop Africa-based artists for international markets.[[102]](#cite_note-110)

## Company value and investments

Big Hit Entertainment initially operated as a private company with Bang Si-hyuk as the largest shareholder of its stock.[[103]](#cite_note-:3-111) In 2018, the Korean mobile gaming company [Netmarble](/wiki/Netmarble_Games "Netmarble Games") acquired the second largest percentage of the company, paying a reported ₩201.4 billion ($191.8 million) for 25.71%.[[104]](#cite_note-112) In October 2018, private investment firm [STIC Investments](/wiki/STIC_Investments "STIC Investments") received an undisclosed share of Big Hit for an investment of roughly ₩104 billion ($93 million).[[105]](#cite_note-113)

In 2007, Big Hit had four employees and was near bankruptcy, but Bang was able to keep the company afloat after the local success of [8Eight](/wiki/8Eight "8Eight")'s *Without a Heart* in 2009.[[106]](#cite_note-114) Over the years, its value rose in tandem with the increasing global popularity of its first boy group BTS.[[107]](#cite_note-115) In March 2018, Big Hit's earnings were publicized for the first time. The company reported revenue of ₩92.4 billion ($82 million) and operating profit of ₩32.5 billion ($29 million) for 2017.[[108]](#cite_note-116)[[109]](#cite_note-theinvestor.co.kr-117) Initial estimates valued Big Hit at up to ₩700 billion ($624 million) if entered into an [IPO](/wiki/Initial_public_offering "Initial public offering") that very month, making CEO Bang the richest in the South Korean entertainment business due to his large shareholding position, and leaving him with personal ownership value of roughly ₩350 billion ($314 million).[[110]](#cite_note-118) By October 2018, the company was valued at more than ₩1 trillion.[[109]](#cite_note-theinvestor.co.kr-117)

Big Hit's success has been attributed to its innovative management style, more like an IT company than an entertainment company, which has become the new standard in the K-pop industry; it includes a wide usage of [social media](/wiki/Social_media "Social media") to capture people's interest and transform it into sales, the creation of artist-related multimedia content, and the full use of [fandom](/wiki/Fandom "Fandom") energy.[[111]](#cite_note-119)[[112]](#cite_note-120) Financials published in March 2019 for 2018, showed a 132% increase in sales compared to 2017, with Big Hit making approximately ₩214.2 billion ($189.38 million). Operating profit rose 97% to ₩64.1 billion ($56.72 million), while net profit rose 105% to ₩50.2 billion ($44.41 million).[[113]](#cite_note-121) By June 2019, the company was valued between ₩1.28–2.22 trillion.[[114]](#cite_note-122) As of March 2020, its total value was roughly ₩6 trillion ($5 billion).[[115]](#cite_note-123) Big Hit commenced plans to go public on May 21, and applied for a pre-IPO consultation with the [Korea Exchange](/wiki/Korea_Exchange "Korea Exchange"). This is required under South Korean law before a company can file paperwork for an IPO.[[116]](#cite_note-124) One week later, the company filed for a preliminary review of their planned IPO.[[117]](#cite_note-125) On October 15, Big Hit was listed in the [KOSPI](/wiki/KOSPI "KOSPI") index and began trading stocks.[[118]](#cite_note-126)[[119]](#cite_note-127)

Per the company's 2020 annual report, its first since going public, Big Hit recorded net income of ₩86.2 billion ($77.6 million), a 19.1% increase compared to 2019's ₩72.4 billion.[[120]](#cite_note-yonhap-128) Operating profit rose 44.3% to ₩142.4 billion, while sales increased 35.6% to ₩796.3 billion[[120]](#cite_note-yonhap-128)—bolstered in part by album revenue and various acquisitions—with the company experiencing its best quarterly performance yet in the fourth quarter of 2020.[[121]](#cite_note-BH_2020-129) Though concert revenue decreased in the wake of the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic_in_South_Korea "COVID-19 pandemic in South Korea"), merchandise, content, and fan club-related revenue increased 53%, 71%, and 66%, respectively.[[121]](#cite_note-BH_2020-129)[[122]](#cite_note-130) In 2021, following its rebranding as Hybe, the company became the first South Korean music agency to surpass the ₩1 trillion ($838 million) benchmark in annual sales. It recorded annual revenue of ₩1.2 trillion, operating profit of ₩190.3 billion, and net income of ₩141 billion ($118 million).[[123]](#cite_note-131)[[124]](#cite_note-132)

#### Tech-driven future growth initiatives

The "tech-driven future growth initiatives" division is made up of specialized business units for video content, [IP](/wiki/Intellectual_property "Intellectual property"), learning, and games. Secondary and tertiary businesses are created based on the creative output of each label.[[125]](#cite_note-MBW_Hybe_presentation-133) In May 2021, Hybe Edu signed a business agreement with the International Korean Language Education Foundation (IKLEF) to develop Korean-language textbooks for distribution to overseas elementary and secondary schools through the Ministry of Education beginning in 2022. Various other online and offline content will also be created using BTS IP in response to an increasing demand for Korean-language education in foreign countries.[[133]](#cite_note-142) In July 2021, it was announced that Hybe Solutions subsidiaries Hybe 360 and Hybe IP had been merged into Hybe.[[134]](#cite_note-143) On June 22, 2022, Cake Corporation announced that Hybe Edu had been bought by them on June 1, but would continue to carry out the activities of the latter.[[135]](#cite_note-144) On July 1, 2025, Hybe IM was renamed to Drimage.[[136]](#cite_note-145)

### Hybe America

Prior to the rebrand, the subsidiary was known as Big Hit America. Following the rebranding, Hybe bought over all ₩1.7 trillion ($1.5 billion) of Big Hit America's shares, making it a wholly owned subsidiary under the corporation, and it became Hybe America.[[53]](#cite_note-KJAD_Ithaca-58) This was in preparation for Hybe's acquisition of Ithaca Holdings through Hybe America.[[e]](#cite_note-Hybe_America-53) BH Odyssey Merger Sub was created as a subsidiary of Hybe America to facilitate its buyout of Ithaca. Once finalized, Ithaca would become a subsidiary of Hybe America, and BH Odyssey would be dissolved.[[53]](#cite_note-KJAD_Ithaca-58)[[49]](#cite_note-Variety_Ithaca-54) The merger is assumed to have been completed in the first quarter of 2021 per quarterly earning reports published on May 14.[[138]](#cite_note-148)

In July 2021, Yoon and Braun were appointed co-CEOs of Hybe America, and jointly managed US operations. Yoon was responsible for "the localization of Hybe's K-pop business model in the US" and "oversee[ing] the training, production and marketing process through which new talent is discovered" while Braun focused "on solidifying Hybe's presence in the US music industry" and "cementing [the company's] competitiveness in the stateside market." Chief Strategy Officer (CSO) Jaesang Lee, who led the Ithaca acquisition, was made Chief Operations Officer (COO).[[139]](#cite_note-149)[[61]](#cite_note-BSH_resign_MBW-66) Plans for the launch of a new global girl group in collaboration with Geffen were announced on November 4; auditions closed on November 28.[[140]](#cite_note-NME_2021_briefing-150)[[141]](#cite_note-151) Braun became the sole CEO in January 2023,[[142]](#cite_note-152) subsequently leading the acquisition of the American entertainment company QC Media Holdings in February.[[143]](#cite_note-153) In July 2025, Braun transitioned out of his role as CEO of Hybe America. Isaac Lee, Chairman of Hybe Latin America, took Braun's place.[[144]](#cite_note-154)

### Hybe Japan

Following Hybe's second leadership restructuring in July 2021, its Japanese subsidiaries, Hybe Solutions Japan and Hybe T&D Japan,[[k]](#cite_note-155) were integrated to form a regional headquarters, Hybe Japan. The Japanese branch operates as an independent entity under CEO and former Hybe Solutions Japan CEO Hyunrock Han.[[145]](#cite_note-156) It oversees music production, music publishing, music copyright management, artist management, and talent scouting and development,[[146]](#cite_note-HybeJapan-157) as well as facilitates the entry of other Hybe artists into the Japanese market.[[60]](#cite_note-Bang_resigns_CEO-65) Hybe Labels Japan debuted its first boy group, [&Team](/wiki/%26Team "&Team"), in December 2022.[[147]](#cite_note-158) A collaboration with [Hiroomi Tosaka](/wiki/Hiroomi_Tosaka "Hiroomi Tosaka") of [J Soul Brothers](/wiki/Sandaime_J_Soul_Brothers "Sandaime J Soul Brothers"), announced in 2021,[[148]](#cite_note-159) resulted in the creation of the girl group [Moonchild](/wiki/Moonchild_(group) "Moonchild (group)"), which debuted in 2023;[[149]](#cite_note-160) Hybe Labels Japan co-produces the group with [LDH Japan](/wiki/LDH_(company) "LDH (company)").[[150]](#cite_note-161) In 2025, Hybe Labels Japan rebranded as YX Labels.[[151]](#cite_note-162) In January 2026, Hybe Japan launched a new label, JCONIC, with the aim of creating icons of the era from Japan.[[152]](#cite_note-:6-163)

### Hybe Latin America

In November 2023, Hybe announced the creation of its [Latin music](/wiki/Latin_music "Latin music") division, **Hybe Latin America**, with offices in [Mexico City](/wiki/Mexico_City "Mexico City"), [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles"), and [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami"). J.H. Kah, former vice president of [YG Entertainment](/wiki/YG_Entertainment "YG Entertainment"), was appointed as [CEO](/wiki/Chief_executive_officer "Chief executive officer"), after the company confirmed the acquisition of Exile Content.[[155]](#cite_note-166) In March 2024, Fernando Grediaga, previously an executive at EMI-[Universal Music México](/wiki/Universal_Music_M%C3%A9xico "Universal Music México"), became general manager of Hybe Latin America.[[156]](#cite_note-167) The leadership team also includes [Isaac Lee](/wiki/Isaac_Lee "Isaac Lee") as chairman and Juan S. Arenas as [COO](/wiki/Chief_operating_officer "Chief operating officer").[[157]](#cite_note-Garcia-168)

In June 2024, Exile Music was rebranded as Zarpazo and relocated to Miami, while its podcast service was renamed Ajá Podcasts.[[158]](#cite_note-169) The establishment of new label Docemil Music was also announced.[[159]](#cite_note-170)

Hybe Latin America will operate the Docemil and Zarpazo brands, with the aim of combining Hybe's K-pop development model with local strategies to target markets in Mexico and the [U.S. Hispanic community](/wiki/Hispanic_and_Latino_Americans "Hispanic and Latino Americans").[[160]](#cite_note-171) The division manages artists including [Morat](/wiki/Morat_(band) "Morat (band)") and [Daddy Yankee](/wiki/Daddy_Yankee "Daddy Yankee") and operates a touring agency for acts such as [Quevedo](/wiki/Quevedo_(singer) "Quevedo (singer)") and [Cypress Hill](/wiki/Cypress_Hill "Cypress Hill").[[157]](#cite_note-Garcia-168)[[161]](#cite_note-172)

Its projects include *[Santos Bravos](/wiki/Santos_Bravos "Santos Bravos")*, a reality show to form an all-male Latin pop group,[[162]](#cite_note-173) and *[Pase a la Fama](/w/index.php?title=Pase_a_la_Fama&action=edit&redlink=1 "Pase a la Fama (page does not exist)")*, a competition in partnership with [Telemundo](/wiki/Telemundo "Telemundo") to select members of a [Regional Mexican](/wiki/Regional_Mexican "Regional Mexican") band.[[163]](#cite_note-174) Additionally, the division launched the Hybe Experience, an interactive exhibition in Mexico City.[[164]](#cite_note-175)

### Hybe China

Hybe China was established on April 2, 2025.[[165]](#cite_note-176)

### Hybe India

On September 23, 2025, Hybe established its Indian subsidiary, *Hybe India Entertainment Pvt. Ltd.*, based in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai").[[166]](#cite_note-177)[[167]](#cite_note-178) The company aims to develop local talent through auditions and training programs, connecting artists born in India to global audiences. Hybe described Mumbai as “a cultural and entertainment hub that encompasses the [Bollywood](/wiki/Hindi_cinema "Hindi cinema") film industry, contemporary art, and diverse performing arts,” unveiling the slogan “*where indian voices become global stories.*” Through its activities in [India](/wiki/India "India"), the subsidiary is also expected to contribute to the growing [I-pop](/wiki/Indian_pop "Indian pop") scene, a developing segment of the Indian music industry.[[168]](#cite_note-179)

## Disputes and legal actions

### Dispute with Min Hee-jin

On April 22, 2024, Hybe requested for [Min Hee-jin](/wiki/Min_Hee-jin "Min Hee-jin"), the then-CEO of their sub-label ADOR, to step down from her position following an [audit](/wiki/Audit "Audit") of the label amidst accusations of Min attempting to make Ador independent.[[170]](#cite_note-181)[[171]](#cite_note-182) Min released a statement dismissing those claims and also accused Hybe of overlooking the similarities between [NewJeans](/wiki/NewJeans "NewJeans") and [Illit](/wiki/Illit "Illit"), a girl group that debuted in March 2024 under Hybe's sub-label Belift Lab. In her statement, she accused Hybe and Belift of refusing to acknowledge the claims of [Illit](/wiki/Illit "Illit") plagiarizing [NewJeans](/wiki/NewJeans "NewJeans")' image from music to style, with the former's debut causing confusion amongst fans online.[[172]](#cite_note-183)[[173]](#cite_note-184) The value of Hybe's shares fell by 8.03% following the news.[[174]](#cite_note-185) In response, Hybe submitted evidence to judicial authorities to refute the claims of plagiarism. A later court case dismissed the plagiarism claims.[[175]](#cite_note-186)

Hybe officially reported Min Hee-jin to the police for "breach of trust" on April 25 following evidence retrieved from the audit that she is attempting to gain management of the label. Hybe later released text messages between Min and ADOR's vice president listing a series of methods to take control of the label. Hybe has also stated that they plan to meet with the legal representatives of NewJeans' members to discuss providing them with psychological and emotional care to support them for a successful return in the future.[[176]](#cite_note-187)[[177]](#cite_note-188)[[178]](#cite_note-:4-189) It was also announced that NewJeans, who are set to release a new single in May, will carry out activities as planned.[[179]](#cite_note-190)

On April 25, seven hours after the report was filed, Min held an emergency press conference to deny the accusations against her. During the conference, she shared text messages exchanged between her, Bang Si-hyuk, and Hybe's former CEO Jiwon Park, some of which included Bang mentioning groups outside of Hybe. She accused Hybe of fabricating her words and revealed that she had filed an internal report in the past because the company failed to address her concerns over their treatment of NewJeans and her as a producer, however, this was ignored by the management.[[180]](#cite_note-:5-191)[[178]](#cite_note-:4-189)[[181]](#cite_note-192) Issues regarding Hybe's leadership also came to light as Min accused Bang of trying to control all of Hybe's labels and removing their uniqueness. Min also stated that she's willing to talk to either Bang or Park but they never agreed.[[180]](#cite_note-:5-191)[[178]](#cite_note-:4-189)

Hybe requested for Ador to organize a board meeting on April 30. If the board members refuse to attend, then a request would be filed in court to open a shareholders' meeting for the label. The company also refuted all of Min's claims, additionally stating that she should respond to the audit and promptly step down from her position.[[178]](#cite_note-:4-189)[[182]](#cite_note-193) Following a court case held on May 30, Min's injunction to stop Hybe from exercising their voting rights to dismiss her was accepted by the court. Meanwhile, the ruling noted that Min had sought to weaken Hybe's control over ADOR by attempting to remove NewJeans from Hybe's influence or by pressuring Hybe to sell its stake in ADOR, thereby enabling herself to take independent control of the label[[183]](#cite_note-194) In respect of the court's decision, Hybe did not exercise its voting rights.[[184]](#cite_note-195) If Hybe were to violate this and fire her, they would have to pay a compensation of [₩](/wiki/South_Korean_won "South Korean won")20 [billion](/wiki/1,000,000,000 "1,000,000,000") ([US$](/wiki/USD "USD")17.48 million).[[185]](#cite_note-196)[[186]](#cite_note-197)

In July 2024, Min filed police complaints against five Hybe executives on charges of defamation and business obstruction. The police ultimately dismissed the case.[[187]](#cite_note-198)

On November 20, 2024, Min resigned from all her positions (including the board internal director) at both ADOR and Hybe.[[188]](#cite_note-199)

On January 9, 2025, Chairman Park Jung-kyu of the tech company Davolink alleged in an interview that Min had made efforts to "poach" NewJeans. He also mentioned that there had been previous discussions between him and Min regarding potential investment and the possibility of NewJeans leaving Ador to sign exclusive contracts with another company.[[189]](#cite_note-200)

In early 2025, Min Hee-jin continued to face scrutiny over allegations of workplace misconduct and breach of trust. In January, the Ministry of Employment and Labor dismissed initial complaints of workplace harassment against her.[[190]](#cite_note-KoreaTimes2025-01-201) However, in March, the Seoul Regional Employment and Labor Office issued an administrative fine against Min, determining that certain actions met the threshold for workplace harassment under South Korean labor law. Min appealed the decision.[[191]](#cite_note-JoongAng2025-03-202)

In June, new developments emerged during ongoing court proceedings between Hybe and Min. Evidence presented in court included claims that Min had a hand in crafting emails that were publicly released by the families of NewJeans (then known as NJZ), raising questions about her role in shaping public narratives.[[192]](#cite_note-TOI2025-06-203)

A major legal decision occurred on July 15, when the Seoul Yongsan Police Station cleared Min of all breach of trust allegations that had been filed by Hybe in April 2024. Authorities concluded there was insufficient evidence to pursue criminal charges.[[193]](#cite_note-KoreaTimes2025-07-204)

Following the decision, Hybe announced its intent to appeal, stating that it had uncovered new evidence and legal grounds to challenge the police's conclusion.[[194]](#cite_note-MBW2025-07-205)[[195]](#cite_note-Reuters-206)

On February 12, 2026, it was reported that the court ruled in favor of Min in both her lawsuit seeking payment for shares under a put option agreement and Hybe's lawsuit seeking confirmation that the shareholder agreement was terminated. After the validity of Min's put option right is finalized, Hybe will have to pay Min ₩25.5 billion (US$17.7 million).[[196]](#cite_note-207)

### Employee insider trading

In June 2024, South Korean prosecutors charged three employees of subsidiaries affiliated with Hybe with insider trading. The individuals were accused of selling company shares shortly before the public announcement on June 14, 2022, that members of the K-pop group BTS would be taking a hiatus. Prosecutors stated that the employees had access to non-public information about the announcement, which enabled them to sell their shares in advance and avoid financial losses estimated between US$24,000 and US$108,000,[[197]](#cite_note-208) which the three denied during their first hearing at trial.[[198]](#cite_note-209)

On July 22, 2025, the three individuals—who were no longer employed at Hybe at the time of sentencing—received suspended prison sentences ranging from six months to two years. In addition to the suspended terms, the court imposed financial penalties ranging from approximately [₩](/wiki/South_Korean_won "South Korean won")51 million ([US$](/wiki/USD "USD")44,582.37) to [₩](/wiki/South_Korean_won "South Korean won")231 million ([US$](/wiki/USD "USD")201,931.9).[[199]](#cite_note-210)[[200]](#cite_note-211)

Separately, on May 29, prosecutors from the Seoul Southern District Prosecutors Office raided the headquarters of Hybe as part of an investigation into allegations of fraudulent trading. A former Hybe executive was accused of earning approximately [₩](/wiki/South_Korean_won "South Korean won")240 million ([US$](/wiki/USD "USD")209,799.38) in profits through insider trading by purchasing shares in [YG Plus](/wiki/YG_Plus "YG Plus") after obtaining non-public information about Hybe's planned investment in the company.[[201]](#cite_note-212)[[202]](#cite_note-213) Hybe stated that it was fully cooperating with authorities and reiterated its commitment to legal compliance and transparency.[[203]](#cite_note-214)

### Leaked audit documents

On October 24, 2024, Hybe came under public scrutiny during a National Assembly audit, when Representative Min Hyung-bae of the Democratic Party questioned Hybe's Chief Operating Officer, Taeho Kim, about the company's internal practices. During the session, Min disclosed the existence of an internal company document titled Weekly Music Industry Report—a collection reportedly totaling approximately 18,000 pages. The report allegedly contained derogatory remarks, unverified rumors, and harsh commentary about various K-pop idols. Portions of the document were later leaked online, prompting widespread criticism.

In response, on October 29, Hybe CEO Jason Jaesang Lee issued a public apology. He described the report as a compilation of external opinions and reactions related to K-pop trends, intended for internal analysis by a limited number of executives. Lee acknowledged that the content was "highly inappropriate and unacceptable" and expressed regret over the harm caused. He stated that Hybe was in the process of directly contacting affected agencies to offer apologies, and he also personally apologized to the company's artists who had been criticized in the leaked material.[[204]](#cite_note-215)[[205]](#cite_note-216)[[206]](#cite_note-217)

### Tax investigation

On July 29, 2025, the National Tax Service (NTS) of South Korea conducted a search and seizure operation at Hybe's headquarters in Yongsan, Seoul. This action was part of a broader investigation by the NTS's 4th Investigation Bureau into alleged tax evasion and irregular financial activities involving 27 companies. Hybe was among the businesses suspected of engaging in unfair stock practices and underreporting tax liabilities.[[207]](#cite_note-TI_-_JoonAng-218)

The probe specifically focused on Hybe's 2020 initial public offering (IPO) and transactions that preceded it. Authorities are investigating claims that Hybe and its executives, including founder and chairman Bang Si-hyuk, misled investors in 2019 by publicly stating that there were no plans for a stock market listing, while secretly preparing for an IPO. The company later proceeded with the listing, and Bang is alleged to have gained hundreds of billions of won in profits through a special purpose vehicle (SPV) that acquired shares on his behalf. Prosecutors believe Bang may have received as much as 30% of the proceeds from this SPV-linked transaction.[[207]](#cite_note-TI_-_JoonAng-218)

This marked the first tax raid on Hybe since June 2022, when a routine audit by the Seoul Regional Tax Office led to the imposition of a "a multi-billion-won penalty."[[208]](#cite_note-219) The 2025 raid, however, signaled a significant escalation, as it followed two previous legal actions within the same month: one involving insider trading by Hybe executives and another centered on IPO-related fraud allegations relating to the founder, Bang Si-hyuk. Combined, these investigations reflect mounting legal and regulatory pressure on the entertainment conglomerate.[[207]](#cite_note-TI_-_JoonAng-218)

## Philanthropy

On February 13, 2023, Hybe donated ₩500 million through Save the Children to support the recovery of victims from [2023 Turkey–Syria earthquake](/wiki/2023_Turkey%E2%80%93Syria_earthquake "2023 Turkey–Syria earthquake").[[221]](#cite_note-232)

On April 2, 2025, Hybe donated ₩1 billion through the [National trust](/wiki/National_trust "National trust") for Cultural Heritage to help repair and restore national heritage damaged by [wildfires](/wiki/2025_South_Korea_wildfires "2025 South Korea wildfires") in the Ulsan, Gyeongbuk, and Gyeongnam regions.[[222]](#cite_note-233)

title: HYBE
content:
HYBE NEB organically integrates content planning, technology, and fan experience to unlock new possibilities in entertainment.   
  We expand HYBE’s original story IP beyond webcomics and webnovels into diverse genres and formats—including music, exhibitions, pop-up spaces, and licensing—enhancing fan immersion through collaborations between artists and characters.  
  HYBE NEB stays ahead of shifts in the entertainment industry through extensive research, identifying future growth opportunities and proposing new areas of content through analysis of evolving customer behavior and technological trends.
 DRIMAGE [...] HYBE BRAND SYNERGY harnesses its planning and execution expertise to drive synergy, building distinctive partnerships between artist IPs and a broad spectrum of domestic and international brands. The team drives initiatives that deliver new experiences and value to fans and the market, including brand ambassador programs, advertising campaigns, collaborations between artists or character IPs and brands, outbound business through HYBE’s business solutions, The City Project—an integrated entertainment platform linked to artist performances—global IP expansion, and the development of lifestyle brands.
 HYBE NEB [...] DRIMAGE is expanding the boundaries of HYBE’s entertainment spectrum by developing games and conducting publishing operations to interact with users worldwide. Through the combination of cutting-edge interactive media technology with music, artists, and entertainment elements, DRIMAGE creates various contents and services that will open doors to new perspectives and experiences.

  View more

# !hybe

Please allow service to collect cookies for a smooth experience with auto login. (**[More](/eng/cookie)**)

# Consent to Collection of Cookies

Cookies are small text files placed on your device which we use to improve your experience on our website and to show you relevant advertising. Our partners and the cookies placed on your device are detailed below. You are also able to manage which cookies are set on your device below. You can change your preferences at any time by clicking on the "Cookie Preferences" icon at the bottom of our website. Your choice will be retained for 12 months. For more information, refer to our Cookie Policy.

* Essential Cookies

  These cookies are strictly necessary for the provision of the service that you have expressly requested or have the sole purpose of enabling or facilitating communication by electronic means. For example, they allow us to remember the items you have placed in your shopping basket. These cookies are automatically activated and cannot be deactivated because they are essential to enable you to browse our site. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
* Analytical/Performance Cookies

  These cookies allow us to understand how you access the site and your browsing habits (e.g. pages viewed, time spent on a page, content clicked). They allow us to analyze the performance and design of our site and to detect possible errors. Owing to these performance indicators, we are constantly improving our site and our products, content and offers. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
* Functionality and Profile Cookies

  These cookies allow us to offer you customized content according to your profile, your interests and your usage. For example, AB test cookies allow us to test different versions of a page/service in order to statistically identify visitor preferences. By accepting these cookies, you help us to improve the layout and features of our site. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
* Advertising / Targeting Cookies

  These cookies collect information about your browsing habits. They remember that you have visited our site and share this information with partners, such as advertisers, for the purpose of targeted advertising. The use of these cookies include displaying advertisements, impression capping, fraud prevention, billing, and measurement. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)

WE BELIEVE IN MUSIC

WHO WE ARE 음악 산업의 비즈니스 모델을 혁신하는 기업 HYBE innovates the music industry business model under the mission, "We believe in music". HYBE strive for the world's best entertainment lifestyle platform company based on music.  
"Content" that lead the global trend and "fans" who are our customers are the two pillars to our values, and we are committed to satisfying our customers by continuously improving our services with the highest standards.

WE  
BELIEVE IN  
MUSIC

We bring change to life by creating resonance across the world and innovation throughout industries.

HYBE is composed of HYBE headquarters in Korea, HYBE AMERICA and HYBE JAPAN. Business division includes Label, Solution and Platform.

LABELS

Each label engages in unique and independent creative activities.

* BIGHIT MUSIC

  BIGHIT MUSIC is an industry-leading music label that continues to pioneer new paths driven by a strong belief in music and ceaseless support from fans. BIGHIT strives to create music heretofore unseen in the world through its inimitable way, creating the music that symbolizes an era that can resonate with fans across the world. Artists under the label includes Lee Hyun, BTS, TOMORROW X TOGETHER and CORTIS.

  [View more](https://ibighit.com/)
* BELIFT LAB

  At BELIFT LAB, we consider it our mission to not only provide fans with the best possible experience but also contribute to innovation in the music industry, building on the foundation of a healthy relationship with our artists. Moreover, we strive to become a new paradigm for K-POP by expanding artist IP beyond the limits of our imagination and providing fans and the music industry with value that goes beyond music. Our artists include ENHYPEN and ILLIT.

  [View more](https://beliftlab.com)
* SOURCE MUSIC

  SOURCE MUSIC is a label with a core value that states, "Music with a clear source and reason can resonate with the listener's emotions." The label has focused on producing high-quality content that can provide a source of joy in life, and aims to become a leader in the global music industry through music business innovation. In collaboration with HYBE, SOURCE MUSIC successfully launched LE SSERAFIM.

  [View more](https://www.sourcemusic.com/)
* PLEDIS Entertainment

  PLEDIS Entertainment's management philosophy is to develop talented artists into shining stars. With years of know-how and extensive networks, PLEDIS is engaged in a diverse range of projects with the goal of expanding the spectrum of popular culture. Artist under the label include BUMZU, HWANG MIN HYUN, SEVENTEEN and TWS. Affiliated companies include Xing Can Sheng Shi (Beijing) Entertainment.

  [View more](https://www.pledis.co.kr/)
* KOZ ENTERTAINMENT

  KOZ ENTERTAINMENT is a unique label that is beloved for pioneering contemporary trends rooted in originality. We create music that resonates with diverse audience, transcending individual personalities and preferences. We present a new paradigm through unique interpretations and creative experiments. Our artists include ZICO and BOYNEXTDOOR.

  [View more](https://www.kozofficial.com/)
* ADOR

  ADOR is a label established in November 2021.  
  ADOR seeks to welcome new challenges with alacrity to engage in exciting and unique businesses. We plan to present different music and projects suggesting a new taste to the market with the new girl group of 2022, NewJeans.

  [View more](https://ador.world/)
* YX LABELS

  YX LABELS aspires to create a new axis in the Japanese music landscape.  
  Guided by the corporate ethos of “Youth, eXtreme, and eXcellence,” YX LABELS deviates from existing methods to pioneer new trends in the Japanese music market, providing an unparalleled entertainment experience. Our artist roster includes &TEAM.

  [View more](http://yxlabels.com/)
* Blue Highway Records

  Blue Highway Records is a Nashville-based country, Americana and roots rock label, representing a premier roster of artists and songwriters at the forefront of the genre. The label builds on an existing foundation, while advancing a distinct creative vision rooted in authenticity, craftsmanship and long-term career growth. Inspired by the spirit of the blue highways that run through the heart of America, Blue Highway Records champions artists whose work reflects lived experience, forging meaningful audience connections and driving the future of country and roots music.   
  Its roster includes marquee talent including Thomas Rhett, Carly Pearce, Brett Young, Midland, Justin Moore, Jackson Dean, Preston Cooper, and Mae Estes.

  [View more](https://bluehighwayrecords.com/)
* QC Music

  Quality Control Music is one of biggest, most influential hip hop labels in the U.S. garnering billions of streams yearly from groundbreaking acts like Migos, Lil Yachty, Lil Baby and City Girls. Founded in West Atlanta by music and entertainment industry pioneers Pierre “P” Thomas and Kevin “Coach K” Lee, QC continues to expand with cutting edge new artists.

  [View more](https://qualitycontrolmusic.com/)
* HYBE x Geffen Records

  In February 2021, HYBE and UMG’s Geffen Records officially announced their strategic joint venture partnership—an unprecedented collaboration between an esteemed U.S. record label and the leading K-pop and entertainment lifestyle platform company, brought together to create the world's first U.S.-based, global girl group modeled on the world-renowned K-pop training & development system. This marked the first time a U.S. record label and a K-pop entertainment company were uniting to combine their expertise in artist discovery and development, and music production to assemble, develop, and introduce a group from ground zero. Their artists include KATSEYE, who made their debut in June 2024 through the global audition show “The Debut: Dream Academy.”
* ZARPAZO

  ZARPAZO is a record label redefining Latin-Iberian music by blending traditional Hispanic rhythms with contemporary influences. It champions independent artists who experiment freely, creating a sound that honors heritage while pushing creative boundaries. Beyond music, ZARPAZO fosters community through dynamic live performances that celebrate culture and connection. By empowering its artists with creative control and promoting an inclusive space for fans and musicians to engage, ZARPAZO challenges stereotypes and reshapes Hispanic identity, leaving a lasting mark on the music world.

  [View more](https://zarpazoentertainment.com/)
* DOCEMIL MUSIC

  DOCEMIL MUSIC is a visionary Latin record label that fuses tradition with innovation, empowering artists to break creative boundaries. More than a label, it’s a cultural movement, building community through live performances and artistic freedom. By redefining Latin identity in music, DOCEMIL MUSIC challenges norms and leaves a lasting global impact.
* S1ENTO Records

  S1ENTO Records, the new label from HYBE Latin America, launches with the mission to discover and elevate the next generation of Música Mexicana artists. We are dedicated to elevating the emerging talent and authenticity of each artist, preparing them for sustainable careers with a global impact. Through 360° storytelling, each project becomes a complete experience that integrates music, narrative, and visuals, designed to connect deeply with the audience's emotions.
* JCONIC

  Under the philosophy of “Creating icons of the era from Japan,” JCONIC develops and produces artists who serve as enduring symbols etched into people's memories. By integrating strategy, production, and management—all rooted in Japan's unique aesthetic sensibility, precision, and philosophy—we ensure that every element, from music and visuals to ideology and presence, is meticulously crafted to achieve iconic status. Our roster includes the artist aoen.

  [View more](https://www.jconiclabels.com)

SOLUTIONS

We provide business solutions to our labels, and drive business initiatives based on music including performances, multimedia content, IP, education and games.

* HYBE 360

  HYBE 360 plans, produces, and operates high-quality content and services—including global tours, fan meetings, the Weverse Con Festival, and showcases—enabling fans around the world to enjoy dynamic performances both online and in person.  
  HYBE 360 leverages a wide range of themes and exhibition content across global locations to strengthen artist branding and enrich in-person fan experiences.
* HYBE IPX

  HYBE IPX develops an extensive lineup of official merchandise, offering fans unique enjoyment and emotional connection through artist IPs, innovation, and character development. Products are distributed globally through online and offline channels, including Weverse, pop-up stores, and concert venues. By leading IP collaborations and global licensing businesses—centered on merchandise and immersive spaces—HYBE IPX helps fans around the world forge deeper connections with their favorite artists and weave meaningful experiences into their daily lives.
* HYBE BRAND SYNERGY

  HYBE BRAND SYNERGY harnesses its planning and execution expertise to drive synergy, building distinctive partnerships between artist IPs and a broad spectrum of domestic and international brands. The team drives initiatives that deliver new experiences and value to fans and the market, including brand ambassador programs, advertising campaigns, collaborations between artists or character IPs and brands, outbound business through HYBE’s business solutions, The City Project—an integrated entertainment platform linked to artist performances—global IP expansion, and the development of lifestyle brands.
* HYBE NEB

  HYBE NEB organically integrates content planning, technology, and fan experience to unlock new possibilities in entertainment.   
  We expand HYBE’s original story IP beyond webcomics and webnovels into diverse genres and formats—including music, exhibitions, pop-up spaces, and licensing—enhancing fan immersion through collaborations between artists and characters.  
  HYBE NEB stays ahead of shifts in the entertainment industry through extensive research, identifying future growth opportunities and proposing new areas of content through analysis of evolving customer behavior and technological trends.
* DRIMAGE

  DRIMAGE is expanding the boundaries of HYBE’s entertainment spectrum by developing games and conducting publishing operations to interact with users worldwide. Through the combination of cutting-edge interactive media technology with music, artists, and entertainment elements, DRIMAGE creates various contents and services that will open doors to new perspectives and experiences.

  [View more](https://drimage.com/)
* HYBE MEDIA STUDIO

  HYBE MEDIA STUDIO plans and produces a variety of original content based on fans and artists, such as fan-oriented content, entertainment content, documentaries, etc. HYBE MEDIA STUDIO provides artist content that captures and delivers the true value and charms of HYBE MUSIC GROUP artists to their fans. HYBE MEDIA STUDIO distributes and commercializes related contents to fandom platforms as well as OTTs and theaters, to expand to a wider global audience.
* Supertone

  Supertone is a leading artificial intelligence (AI) audio technology company that empowers creators to craft more immersive and engaging content through innovative solutions.  
  Its AI-powered voice technology delivers hyper-realistic voices capable of both acting and singing, revolutionizing the creative process from sound generation to fan engagement.  
  Supertone’s AI solutions are applied across a wide range of content domains—including music, film, YouTube, and VTubers—breaking communication barriers and shaping a future where creators and artists can connect more closely with their fans.

  [View more](https://supertone.ai/)
* Scooter Braun Projects

  Founded by veteran music entrepreneur and HYBE America CEO Scooter Braun, Scooter Braun Projects is a talent management company that has an established track record of helping its clients achieve worldwide fame and commercial success, including Ariana Grande, Black Eyed Peas, CL, Dan + Shay, Demi Lovato, Idina Menzel, J Balvin, Justin Bieber, The Knocks, Lil Dicky, Tori Kelly, and more.

  [View more](https://scooterbraun.com/)
* Solid Foundation Management

  Solid Foundation Management offers services ranging from contract negotiation, strategy, to branding and marketing solutions. Solid Foundation has been responsible for managing and guiding the careers of world class talent from its inception like Migos, Lil’ Yachty, Lil Baby and City Girls.
* Big Machine Music

  Nashville-based Big Machine Music, part of Big Machine Label Group, is a full-service independent music publisher with a dynamic catalog of over 12,000 songs, nearly 50 No. 1s and dozens of hits on music charts around the world.

  [View more](https://www.bigmachinemusic.com/)

We connect and expand all HYBE content and services.

* WEVERSE COMPANY

  Weverse Company is a global fandom platform company, dedicated to innovating fandom culture through expansion of customer experience.   
  “Weverse” is an all-in-one platform that brings together every service artists and fans need, allowing fans to enjoy real-time communication with artists, concert streaming, content viewing, and shopping within a single space that integrates community, content, and commerce.   
  Weverse continues to evolve into a global super fan platform by shaping a seamless fandom experience through ongoing service innovations, including “Jelly,” the official in-app payment method, “Weverse DM,” a private messaging service that enables direct communication between fans and artists, and “Listening Party,” which invites users listen to music together and communicate in real-time.

  [View more](https://weverse.co/)

HYBE has established and operates   
 the following framework to uphold   
 ethical management practices.

  + 09월

  + 09월

  + 09월

    세븐틴, 일 도쿄 돔 10만 명 열광…데뷔 후 최대 규모 5개 도시 돔 투어 스타트!
  + 09월

  + 09월

  + 09월

  + 09월

* 기업형태: 1000대기업, 대기업, 코스피, 주식회사
* **905 명** 

  출처: 국민연금

업종
:   음악 및 기타 오디오물 출판업

대표자명
:   이재상

    * [트위터](https://twitter.com/BigHitEnt "트위터 새창으로 이동")

**WE BELIEVE IN MUSIC**

하이브(HYBE)는 “We believe in music”이라는 미션 아래 음악 산업의 비즈니스 모델을 혁신하는 기업입니다.   
 하이브는 음악에 기반한 세계 최고의 엔터테인먼트 라이프스타일 플랫폼 기업을 지향합니다.  
 글로벌 트렌드를 이끄는 '콘텐츠'와 우리의 고객인 '팬'을 최우선 가치로 두고, 높은 기준과 끊임없는 개선으로 고객을 만족시키도록 노력하고 있습니다.

**기업비전**

WE BELIEVE IN MUSIC  
 음악으로 세상에 울림을 전하고 산업을 혁신하여 삶의 변화를 만들어갑니다.

NICE 평가정보 출처 : 설립일, 기업형태, 업종, 대표자명, 사업내용, 주소  
 국민연금 출처 : 사원수  
 기업 직접입력 : 홈페이지, 브랜드명, SNS, 기업 한줄소개, 기업소개, 기업비전

# !hybe

Please allow service to collect cookies for a smooth experience with auto login. (**[More](/eng/cookie)**)

# Consent to Collection of Cookies

Cookies are small text files placed on your device which we use to improve your experience on our website and to show you relevant advertising. Our partners and the cookies placed on your device are detailed below. You are also able to manage which cookies are set on your device below. You can change your preferences at any time by clicking on the "Cookie Preferences" icon at the bottom of our website. Your choice will be retained for 12 months. For more information, refer to our Cookie Policy.

* Essential Cookies

  These cookies are strictly necessary for the provision of the service that you have expressly requested or have the sole purpose of enabling or facilitating communication by electronic means. For example, they allow us to remember the items you have placed in your shopping basket. These cookies are automatically activated and cannot be deactivated because they are essential to enable you to browse our site. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
* Analytical/Performance Cookies

  These cookies allow us to understand how you access the site and your browsing habits (e.g. pages viewed, time spent on a page, content clicked). They allow us to analyze the performance and design of our site and to detect possible errors. Owing to these performance indicators, we are constantly improving our site and our products, content and offers. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
* Functionality and Profile Cookies

  These cookies allow us to offer you customized content according to your profile, your interests and your usage. For example, AB test cookies allow us to test different versions of a page/service in order to statistically identify visitor preferences. By accepting these cookies, you help us to improve the layout and features of our site. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
* Advertising / Targeting Cookies

  These cookies collect information about your browsing habits. They remember that you have visited our site and share this information with partners, such as advertisers, for the purpose of targeted advertising. The use of these cookies include displaying advertisements, impression capping, fraud prevention, billing, and measurement. [VIEW COOKIES](/eng/cookie)
